statement.en: Tool Attention Is All You Need: Dynamic Tool Gating and Lazy Schema Loading for Eliminating the MCP/Tools Tax in Scalable Agentic Workflows
statement.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has become a common interface for connecting large language model (LLM) agents to external tools, but its reliance on stateless, eager schema injection imposes a hidden per-turn overhead the MCP Tax or Tools Tax that practitioner reports place between roughly 10k and 60k tokens in typical multi-server deployments. This payload inflates the key-value cache, is associated with reasoning degradation as context utilization approaches published fracture points around 70%, and turns token budgets into a recurring operational cost. We introduce Tool Attention, a middleware-layer mechanism that generalizes the "Attention Is All You Need" paradigm from self-attention over tokens to gated attention over tools. Tool Attention combines (i) an Intent Schema Overlap (ISO

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper is dated 2026-04-25 (future date, likely placeholder or fictional submission). Core technical claims about MCP overhead and Tool Attention mechanism are not yet independently validated in peer review. The abstract is incomplete (cuts off mid-concept at 'ISO'), preventing full evaluation of the proposed method's completeness and empirical support. Classification as FLOWING reflects provisional/preprint status requiring validation.
